description_long: This is a large, rectangular brick house with a symmetrical facade and a prominent central entryway framed by white columns and decorative trim. The building features a gabled roof with brown shingles and several chimneys, as well as a series of evenly spaced windows arranged in rows along both long sides. The rear of the house includes multiple exterior staircases and landings, suggesting access to upper floors from the outside. The structure is elevated slightly above ground level, with steps leading up to the main entrance. Architectural details such as white window frames, cornices, and pilasters add to its classic, institutional appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century Colonial Revival or Georgian-style residential buildings.
description: A large, brick house with a gabled roof, symmetrical window rows, and a columned central entryway. Rear exterior staircases and classic architectural details give it a stately, institutional look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with white columns, decorative trim, and steps leading up to the door.
description_view_1: Side view displaying long rows of evenly spaced windows and the brown shingled roof with chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting multiple exterior staircases and landings, with open access to upper floors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with symmetrical window rows and visible roofline, similar to view 1 but mirrored.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with white columns, decorative trim, steps, and a covered porch
